mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 6.0%, it signals a deteriorating labor market that reduces consumer spending capacity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 6.0%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) during high-unemployment periods.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in retail sales and a decrease in the federal funds rate indicates strong consumer demand supported by accommodative policy, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS rises and FEDFUNDS falls, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production signals rising corporate output and capacity utilization, which drives positive short-term S&P 500 returns as earnings expectations adjust upward.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, or the signal reverses direction when inflation exceeds 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth reflects expanding economic activity and corporate revenue streams, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter as earnings forecasts are revised upward.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows no statistical relationship with 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL accelerates past the threshold, or the signal loses predictive accuracy (Rank IC < 0.01) during high-inflation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and housing starts indicates synchronized labor market strength and durable goods demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and HOUST growth signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts signal increased construction activity and supply chain demand, generating positive short-term S&P 500 returns as industrial and materials sectors anticipate higher order volumes.
failure_condition: HOUST increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during low-rate periods, or the signal reverses when mortgage rates exceed 7.0%.
